title: arabis mollis subsp. graellsiiformis
intro: Arabis mollis subsp. graellsiiformis, a lesser-known subspecies of the Arabis mollis plant, is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its delicate white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Arabis mollis subsp. graellsiiformis has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing coughs, due to its high content of flavonoids and terpenes. The herbal preparations of Arabis mollis subsp. graellsiiformis include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or taken internally, depending on the specific health condition being addressed.
